commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch threads outbound `sessionKey` into `message_sending` and `message_sent`, aligns native redirect routed replies with the agent runtime session key, documents the hook/session contract, and adds tests, a proof script, API hash refresh, and changelog text.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main is source-reproducible: outbound delivery computes a canonical hook session key but does not pass it to `message_sending`, and native redirected replies pass the inbound key even though the agent runtime uses the redirect target key.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can adjust the no-session `message_sending` context shape and strengthen the focused regression test; no product or security decision is needed.

target.title: fix(outbound): thread sessionKey into message_sending + align session.key with agent runtime + document the contract
target.author: zeroaltitude

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: zeroaltitude
commands.command: please re-review. round 4 follow-up commit `862bb66603` addressed all findings: (1) threaded canonical `sessionkey` into `message_sent` hook context symmetrically with `message_sending`; (2) narrowed jsdoc on `runid` to honestly reflect inbound-only plumbing today; (3) narrowed jsdoc on `sessionkey` to note omission on internal smoke runs; (4) added two new pinned contract tests for `message_sent` sessionkey; (5) added changelog entry; (6) added two dispatch-from-config regression tests covering native vs. non-native session-key routing. real-runtime proof updated in prior comment, all 3 scenarios verified against production hook runner (no mocks).
